body.title: Escalation eval corpus — graduated queries with ground-truth verifiers; measures each model's real rung
body.description:
'We build out a set of queries that lower level models will just have to escalate on. Once that works, we come back to this.' (Akien, 2026-07-09).

This is the missing LEVER. `difficulty_bucket` is a hand-typed claim (gemini-2.0-flash is labelled `design`; claude-sonnet-4.6 is labelled `code`), and the cost-first selector REWARDS overclaiming (T-inference-cost-first-sort-strands-cloud-fleet). A corpus of queries with deterministic, checkable answers turns that claim into a MEASUREMENT: run every query against every model, record pass/fail, and the resulting pass-frontier IS the model's real capability rung.

WORK:
- A corpus of queries graduated across difficulty, each with a DETERMINISTIC verifier (exact match, numeric tolerance, or a regex on a canonical form). No LLM judge in the loop — a judge is itself a model whose rung is unmeasured.
- Graduation must span the whole reachable+unreachable fleet, so the corpus can show WHERE the ladder tops out: easy (llama3.2:3b passes), mid (deepseek-r1:14b passes, 3b fails), hard (deepseek-r1:32b passes, 14b fails), very hard (NO local model passes; only a frontier cloud model does).
- The 'very hard' band is load-bearing: it is the band that proves the local-only ladder cannot answer it, which is the evidence T-inference-cost-first-sort-strands-cloud-fleet and T-inference-tier-ladder-real need.
- Output: a capability matrix (model x query-band -> pass rate) written to the memory store as a note/proof artifact, greppable and dated. This becomes the input to a MEASURED difficulty_bucket / capability rank.

SCOPE DISCIPLINE (from the advisor, and from the 2026-07-08 bad close):
- The corpus + verifiers are a HERMETIC unit (pure data + pure functions). Test them hermetically.
- The LIVE run against real models is a SMOKE RUN producing DATA, not a green assertion. Do NOT assert 'escalation reached a capable model' through a live InferenceDevice() — provider health makes that pass or fail on the weather.
- EXPECT the ladder to top out below the hard band. That is not a mechanism bug; it is the eval doing its job. Read a top-rung failure as label-data.

**Test plan:** red->green, hermetic. Each verifier accepts the known-good answer and rejects a plausible confabulation (the corpus must reject a confident wrong answer, not just an empty one). Corpus is non-empty in every band and every entry has a verifier. Separately, a live smoke script emits the capability matrix; its output is recorded, never asserted.

**Decision:** decision:D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations-2026-07-08

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: 12 graduated queries in 4 structural bands, each with a deterministic verifier and the plausible confabulation it must reject. Proven: proofs/cc.0.escalation-eval-corpus.T-inference-escalation-eval-corpus.20260709.080435027297.json (red=AssertionError -> green=passed). No LLM judge. Live capability matrix is a separate smoke run (data, not assertion).
